notes.note: Cicero's joint portrait of the consuls of 58 BC, A. Gabinius and L. Calpurnius Piso Caesoninus, the men under whom he went into exile; the figure of two whirlwinds (procellae) recurs in In Pisonem (55 BC).

"Peace with standing" --- Cicero's famous formula (sections 96--98) for the political programme of the optimate establishment: peace not as absence of conflict but as the standing of the senatorial order maintained.

Cicero distinguishes the contio (the assembly as it gathers in the Forum, where he claims tribunician demagogues hold the upper hand) from the populus (the people in their voting tribes). A constitutional figure that recurs in the late-Republican optimate vocabulary.

Clodius Aesopus, the most famous tragic actor of his generation; cited as having spoken from the stage of Accius's Eurysaces and Brutus in such a way as to point at Cicero's exile, his audience taking the dramatic verses as political commentary.
